key_insight: A polygonal cell-based smoothed finite element framework can robustly handle nonlinear geotechnical problems while reducing mesh sensitivity and improving geometric flexibility.
engineering_relevance: This is useful for geotechnical design and analysis where complex boundaries, staged excavation, local failure, and mixed mesh quality are common.
method: Uses Wachspress interpolation on polygonal elements.
Applies cell-based strain smoothing with boundary integration over subcells.
Implements incremental elasto-plastic updates for Mohr-Coulomb and Duncan-Chang soil models in ABAQUS via UEL.
evidence_signal: The method was verified on benchmark and engineering cases including a perforated plate, strip footing, core rockfill dam, tunnel excavation, and slope stability, showing accurate displacement, stress, plastic strain, bearing capacity, and factor of safety predictions.
watch_item: Performance depends on careful implementation of the UEL, smoothing subcell construction, and constitutive update stability for highly nonlinear or softening soil responses.
paper_title: Nonlinear Geotechnical Analysis Using a Polygonal Cell-Based Smoothed Finite Element Framework
